description: 500 pre-1920 historic mines on public land in Northern and Eastern regions

mines.name: Treadwell Mine
mines.description: Alaska's first major gold mine producing over 3 million ounces of gold 1881-1917. Operated by Alaska Treadwell Gold Mining Company until catastrophic seawater cave-in. Extensive surface ruins and collapsed workings accessible via public trails near Douglas Island south of Juneau.

mines.name: Alaska-Juneau Mine
mines.description: Major low-grade gold operation processing 90 million tons of ore 1893-1944. Mill complex ruins visible from the Perseverance Trail managed by City and Borough of Juneau as public open space.

mines.name: Ready Bullion Mine
mines.description: Part of the Treadwell group on Douglas Island producing gold until the 1917 seawater flooding. Ruins accessible via Treadwell Historic Trail on City and Borough of Juneau public land.

mines.name: Mexican Mine
mines.description: One of four major Treadwell group mines on Douglas Island processing low-grade gold ore. Ceased operations in the 1917 cave-in. Historic ruins on City and Borough of Juneau public land.

mines.name: 700-Foot Mine
mines.description: Third Treadwell group mine on Douglas Island named for its main level depth. Operated until 1917 when seawater flooded all four Treadwell mines simultaneously. Public access via Treadwell Historic Trail.

mines.name: Perseverance Mine
mines.description: Historic gold mine at the head of Gold Creek above Juneau operated 1885 through early 1920s. Accessible via Perseverance Trail through historic Silverbow Basin managed by City and Borough of Juneau.

mines.name: Ebner Mine
mines.description: Early gold lode mine on slopes above Juneau near Snowslide Gulch operated intermittently 1887 through early 1920s. Located on Tongass National Forest land with remnant waste rock piles visible from hiking trails.

mines.name: Last Chance Mine
mines.description: Gold lode mine in Silverbow Basin above Juneau part of the upper Gold Creek mining district. Operated during the height of the Juneau gold rush on Tongass National Forest land.

mines.name: Sheep Creek Mine
mines.description: Gold-silver lode mine on Sheep Creek south of Juneau part of the extended Treadwell mineralized belt. Mill site with ore car remnants on National Forest land accessible from Sheep Creek Trail.

mines.name: Silver Bow Basin Mine
mines.description: Historic mine workings in Silver Bow Basin at headwaters of Gold Creek above Juneau. Multiple small claims worked 1880s-1910s on Tongass National Forest land at end of Perseverance Trail.

mines.name: Gold Creek Placer Claims
mines.description: Original placer gold discovery by Joe Juneau and Richard Harris in October 1880 triggering the Juneau gold rush. Historic site on City and Borough of Juneau land in Flume Park area.

mines.name: Granite Creek Mine (Juneau)
mines.description: Gold-quartz vein mine on Granite Creek near Juneau operated intermittently 1891 through 1920s. Located on Tongass National Forest land with access from lower basin trails.

mines.name: Heintzleman Mine
mines.description: Developed lode gold mine in upper Gold Creek watershed above Juneau operated early 20th century. Located on Tongass National Forest land above Perseverance Trail terminus.

mines.name: Thane Mine
mines.description: Gold lode mine at Thane south of Juneau along Gastineau Channel worked 1912-1920s. Mill ruins still visible at old Thane townsite on National Forest land accessible by road.

mines.name: Gastineau Channel Placers
mines.description: Beach placer gold workings along east shore of Gastineau Channel worked seasonally during low tide following discovery of placer gold from Treadwell tailings. Active 1880s-1910s near Juneau.

mines.name: Lemon Creek Mine
mines.description: Early placer and lode gold mine on Lemon Creek northwest of Juneau operated intermittently from the 1890s. Named after an early miner. Located on Tongass National Forest land.

mines.name: Windfall Lake Mine
mines.description: Gold placer and lode mine near Windfall Lake north of Juneau in the Herbert River valley. Operated early 20th century on Tongass National Forest land.

mines.name: Sheep Creek Hydraulic
mines.description: Hydraulic placer gold operation on Sheep Creek south of Juneau using high-pressure water jets on gold-bearing gravels. Operated 1893-1910s on National Forest land.

mines.name: Gold Run Mine (Juneau)
mines.description: Placer and lode gold mine on Gold Run tributary of Gold Creek above Juneau. Active during first decade of the Juneau gold rush on City of Juneau public land.

mines.name: Kowee Creek Mine
mines.description: Named for Tlingit leader Kowee who guided early prospectors to gold. Gold placer mine on Kowee Creek Douglas Island active during the early Treadwell era on City of Douglas public land.

mines.name: Bonanza Mine (Juneau)
mines.description: Early Juneau district gold lode mine on the mineral-bearing formation above Gold Creek operated during the 1890s on Tongass National Forest land.

mines.name: Empire Mine (Juneau)
mines.description: Lode gold mine on high slopes above Juneau in the Silver Bow Basin area worked 1890s-1910s. Located on Tongass National Forest land.

mines.name: Montana Creek Mine (Juneau)
mines.description: Placer gold mine on Montana Creek north of Juneau active during late 1890s gold rush era. Located on Tongass National Forest land in the Mendenhall Valley area.

mines.name: Basin Road Mine
mines.description: Lode gold mine along Gold Creek accessed from Basin Road above Juneau worked 1880s-1920s. Located on City and Borough of Juneau public land above Last Chance Basin.

mines.name: Annex Creek Mine
mines.description: Gold lode mine on Annex Creek south of Juneau near the Taku Inlet area operated in the early 20th century on National Forest land.

mines.name: Clear Creek Mine (Juneau)
mines.description: Gold placer mine on Clear Creek on east slope of Douglas Island near the Treadwell complex. Operated 1890s as part of the greater Treadwell mining activity on City of Douglas public land.

mines.name: Alaska-Pacific Mine
mines.description: Gold-silver lode mine west of the Juneau district operated in the early 20th century. Located on Tongass National Forest land in the mountains west of Juneau.

mines.name: Douglas Island Placers
mines.description: Placer gold workings along beaches and creek bottoms of Douglas Island worked in connection with Treadwell milling tailings discharge. Active 1880s-1910s south of Juneau.

mines.name: Ptarmigan Creek Mine
mines.description: Gold-quartz lode mine on Ptarmigan Creek in the high basin above Juneau worked in the early 20th century. Remote location on Tongass National Forest land.

mines.name: Salmon Creek Mine (Juneau)
mines.description: Gold and silver mine on Salmon Creek west of Juneau in the Coast Mountains. Operated intermittently 1898-1920 on Tongass National Forest land near the Salmon Creek reservoir.

mines.name: Nome Beach Placers
mines.description: Famous beach placer discovery at Nome triggering the 1899 gold rush bringing 20,000 miners. The black sand beaches at Nome carried extraordinarily rich gold deposits along the Seward Peninsula Bering Sea coast on BLM land.

mines.name: Anvil Creek Mine
mines.description: Original gold discovery on Anvil Creek by the three lucky Swedes in September 1898, the richest creek placer in the Nome district yielding millions in gold. Located on BLM land near Anvil Mountain above Nome.

mines.name: Glacier Creek Mine (Nome)
mines.description: Major placer gold creek in the Nome district on the south slope of the Kigluaik Mountains. Rich creek gravels and benches worked by dredges and hydraulic operations 1899-1930s on BLM Seward Peninsula land.

mines.name: Ophir Creek Mine (Nome)
mines.description: Important placer creek in the Nome district flowing to the Snake River. Rich deep bench placers and creek gravels yielded gold 1900-1920s. BLM public land near Nome Alaska.

mines.name: Snow Gulch Mine
mines.description: Placer gold mine on Snow Gulch a tributary in the Nome district. Active during the Nome gold rush era with individual and dredge operations on BLM Seward Peninsula land.

mines.name: Nome Dredge Field
mines.description: Gold dredge operation site on tundra plains near Nome processing deep placer deposits. One of multiple large dredges working the Nome plain 1910-1940s. BLM land; dredge pond scars remain on the tundra.

mines.name: Rock Creek Mine (Nome)
mines.description: Significant gold placer creek near Nome with documented production. Worked by individual miners and later by dredge on BLM Seward Peninsula land near Nome Alaska.

mines.name: Big Creek Mine (Nome)
mines.description: Large drainage placer gold mine near Nome with substantial creek and bench deposits. Active 1900-1930 on BLM Seward Peninsula land.

mines.name: Gold Run Creek Mine (Nome)
mines.description: Placer gold mine on Gold Run Creek part of the Snake River drainage system near Nome. Active during the Nome rush era on BLM public land.

mines.name: Kougarok River Mine
mines.description: Placer gold mine on the Kougarok River north of Nome in the Seward Peninsula interior. Rich placer gold deposits worked 1901-1930 on BLM public land.

mines.name: Council City Placers
mines.description: One of the earliest gold discoveries on the Seward Peninsula predating the Nome beach find. Rich placer gold on Fish River near Council AK produced significant gold 1897-1920 on BLM land.

mines.name: Nome Second Beach Line
mines.description: Ancient beach placer gold deposits behind the modern Nome beach representing an older Bering Sea shoreline. Worked by hydraulic and steam thaw methods 1901-1920 on BLM land.

mines.name: Candle Creek Mine
mines.description: Gold placer mine on Candle Creek part of the Seward Peninsula gold fields east of Nome. Active early 20th century on BLM land.

mines.name: Ophir Creek Mine (Seward Peninsula)
mines.description: Rich placer gold mine on Ophir Creek in the Council area of the Seward Peninsula. Active during the 1898-1910 Council district rush on BLM public land.

mines.name: Melsing Creek Mine
mines.description: Placer gold creek mine near Council City Seward Peninsula part of the early Council district rush before Nome became dominant. Active on BLM land.

mines.name: Bourbon Creek Mine (Nome)
mines.description: Placer gold creek mine in the Nome district part of the rich western drainage flowing toward Nome. Active first decade of the 20th century on BLM Seward Peninsula land.

mines.name: Penny River Mine
mines.description: Placer gold mine on the Penny River east of Nome worked during the Nome gold rush era on BLM Seward Peninsula land.

mines.name: Dry Creek Mine (Nome)
mines.description: Placer gold mine on Dry Creek near Nome worked with hydraulic and ground sluice methods 1901-1920 on BLM land.

mines.name: Dexter Creek Mine
mines.description: Placer gold creek mine east of Nome in the Kigluaik foothills active during the Nome rush. BLM Seward Peninsula public land.

mines.name: Sinuk River Mine
mines.description: Placer gold mine on the Sinuk River west of Nome part of the extended Nome district on BLM Seward Peninsula land.

mines.name: Pedro Creek Discovery Mine
mines.description: Felix Pedro's 1902 gold discovery on Pedro Creek founding the Fairbanks mining district, the largest gold district in Alaska's interior. Pedro Creek produced millions in placer gold 1902-1940 on BLM land north of Fairbanks.

mines.name: Cleary Creek Mine
mines.description: One of the richest creeks in the Fairbanks district discovered shortly after Pedro Creek in 1902. Produced millions in placer gold continuously through the 1930s by dredge and hydraulic methods on BLM land north of Fairbanks.

mines.name: Fairbanks Creek Mine
mines.description: Major placer gold creek in the Fairbanks district discovered 1903 and extensively dredged 1910-1930s. Large dredge pond scars remain on BLM land in the Fairbanks district.

mines.name: Ester Creek Mine
mines.description: Rich placer gold creek west of Fairbanks in the Ester dome area heavily dredged 1910-1940s. Gold Dredge No. 8 on this creek is now a National Historic Landmark and tourist attraction on BLM land.

mines.name: Gold Dredge No. 8 Site
mines.description: Historic Bucket Line Dredge No. 8 on Goldstream Creek north of Fairbanks operated 1928-1959. Now a National Historic Landmark open for tours; processed millions of cubic yards of gold-bearing gravel on BLM/State land.

mines.name: Goldstream Creek Mine
mines.description: Major placer gold creek north of Fairbanks heavily worked by multiple dredges 1910-1960 and one of the most productive streams in the Fairbanks district. BLM land; dredge ponds still visible.

mines.name: Vault Creek Mine
mines.description: Placer gold creek in the Fairbanks district north of Dome Creek active 1904 through 1930s with hand and dredge operations on BLM land.

mines.name: Dome Creek Mine (Fairbanks)
mines.description: Placer gold creek in the Fairbanks district near Pedro Dome active 1903-1930s. Named for Pedro Dome the high point overlooking the district. Located on BLM land.

mines.name: Livengood Creek Mine
mines.description: Rich placer gold discovery at Livengood north of Fairbanks in 1914 creating a minor boom. Dredged extensively 1920s-1940s on BLM land in the Tolovana district.

mines.name: Tolovana Placers
mines.description: Placer gold fields on Livengood Creek and Tolovana River northwest of Fairbanks discovered 1914-1915 producing millions in gold through the 1930s on BLM land in the Tolovana district.

mines.name: Fox Creek Mine (Fairbanks)
mines.description: Placer gold mine on Fox Creek near the community of Fox north of Fairbanks active during the Fairbanks district boom on BLM/State land.

mines.name: Nugget Creek Mine (Fairbanks)
mines.description: Placer gold mine on Nugget Creek near Pedro Creek in the Fairbanks district active from the first years of the Fairbanks rush on BLM land.

mines.name: Engineer Creek Mine (Fairbanks)
mines.description: Placer gold mine on Engineer Creek in the Fairbanks district active during the boom era on BLM land.

mines.name: Fish Creek Mine (Fairbanks)
mines.description: Placer gold mine on Fish Creek in the northern Fairbanks district worked by hand and hydraulic methods 1904-1920s on BLM land.

mines.name: Ready Bullion Creek Mine
mines.description: Placer gold mine on Ready Bullion Creek in the Fairbanks district active during the gold rush era 1904-1930s on BLM land.

mines.name: Champion Creek Mine (Fairbanks)
mines.description: Placer gold mine on Champion Creek in the Fairbanks district worked during the height of the Fairbanks boom on BLM land.

mines.name: Twin Creek Mine (Fairbanks)
mines.description: Placer gold mine on Twin Creek in the Fairbanks district worked during the early 20th century boom era on BLM land.

mines.name: Little Eldorado Creek Mine
mines.description: Placer gold mine on Little Eldorado Creek in the Fairbanks district active during the gold rush era on BLM land.

mines.name: California Creek Mine (Fairbanks)
mines.description: Placer gold mine on California Creek in the Fairbanks district active in the early 20th century on BLM land.

mines.name: Flat Creek Mine (Iditarod)
mines.description: Rich placer gold discovery at Flat Creek near the Iditarod River in 1908 triggering the Iditarod gold rush. The Flat district produced millions in gold through the 1930s on BLM land in western Alaska interior.

mines.name: Iditarod River Placers
mines.description: Extensive placer gold deposits along the Iditarod River in western Alaska interior. One of the last major gold rush areas in Alaska before WWI on BLM land; the historic Iditarod Trail follows this route.

mines.name: Otter Creek Mine (Iditarod)
mines.description: Placer gold mine on Otter Creek near Flat in the Iditarod district active during the 1909-1920 Iditarod rush on BLM land in the Kilbuck Mountains area.

mines.name: Flat Townsite Mine
mines.description: Mine workings surrounding the Flat townsite in the Iditarod district. The townsite served surrounding placer camps 1908-1930s on BLM land; historic townsite ruins remain.

mines.name: Ophir Townsite Mine
mines.description: Mine workings at the ghost town of Ophir in the Innoko district an active placer gold camp 1906-1920s along the Iditarod Trail on BLM land; ghost town ruins remain.

mines.name: Innoko River Placers
mines.description: Placer gold mines along the Innoko River in the Innoko district of western interior Alaska active 1906-1930s producing gold from creek and river deposits on BLM land.

mines.name: Donlin Creek Mine
mines.description: Historic gold deposits at Donlin Creek in the Kuskokwim Mountains worked as placer and lode gold claims during the Iditarod-area boom. Historic workings date to 1909 on BLM land.

mines.name: Discovery Creek Mine (Iditarod)
mines.description: Original discovery point of the Iditarod gold field. Placer gold found 1908 setting off the rush to the Flat area on BLM land in the Iditarod drainage.

mines.name: Long Creek Mine (Iditarod)
mines.description: Placer gold mine on Long Creek in the Flat/Iditarod district active during the Iditarod rush era on BLM land.

mines.name: Pioneer Creek Mine (Iditarod)
mines.description: Placer gold mine on Pioneer Creek in the Iditarod mining district active in the early 20th century on BLM land.

mines.name: Ganes Creek Mine
mines.description: Placer gold mine on Ganes Creek in the Kusilvak Mountains east of the Iditarod district. Active during the early 20th century interior Alaska placer rush on BLM land.

mines.name: McGrath Area Placers
mines.description: Placer gold deposits near McGrath on the Kuskokwim River in interior Alaska part of the extended Iditarod-Kuskokwim gold district on BLM land.

mines.name: Kennecott Copper Mine
mines.description: One of the richest copper deposits ever mined producing over 4.6 billion pounds of copper 1903-1938. The historic mill town is a National Historic Landmark within Wrangell-St. Elias National Park open to visitors.

mines.name: Bonanza Mine (Kennecott)
mines.description: Original discovery claim at Kennecott found by Jack Smith and Clarence Warner in 1900 proving to be one of the world's richest copper deposits. Located within Wrangell-St. Elias National Park.

mines.name: Jumbo Mine (Kennecott)
mines.description: Second major copper mine in the Kennecott group producing high-grade chalcocite copper ore from above 7,000 feet elevation. Tramway connected to mill town within Wrangell-St. Elias National Park.

mines.name: Mother Lode Mine (Kennecott)
mines.description: Third Kennecott group mine operating on a separate ore body at high elevation above the Kennecott Valley. Ore transported to mill via aerial tramway in Wrangell-St. Elias National Park.

mines.name: Erie Mine (Kennecott)
mines.description: Part of the Kennecott copper mining group worked 1911-1930s within Wrangell-St. Elias National Park public land.

mines.name: McCarthy Creek Mine
mines.description: Placer and lode gold mine on McCarthy Creek near the Kennecott copper mines. McCarthy served as the supply town for Kennecott on BLM/Wrangell-St. Elias NP land.

mines.name: Chisana Placers
mines.description: Last major Alaska gold rush at Chisana in 1913 bringing thousands of stampeders to the upper White River country. Rich placer gold on Bonanza Creek and tributaries in Wrangell-St. Elias National Park.

mines.name: Nizina River Placers
mines.description: Early placer gold deposits along the Nizina River in the Wrangell Mountains predating the Kennecott copper discovery. Worked by early Alaskan prospectors in Wrangell-St. Elias National Park.

mines.name: Miller Creek Mine (Circle)
mines.description: Early Yukon-area placer gold mine on Miller Creek in the Circle district one of the first significant Alaska gold strikes before the Klondike rush. Rich placer gold creek active 1893-1910s on BLM land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - BLM Alaska Circle District

mines.name: Birch Creek Placers
mines.description: Placer gold fields along Birch Creek in the Circle district among the first major Alaska gold discoveries. The Birch Creek drainage produced gold 1893-1920s on BLM land in the Yukon Flats area.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - BLM Alaska Circle District

mines.name: Mastodon Creek Mine
mines.description: Important placer gold creek in the Circle district named for mastodon fossils found in the gold-bearing gravels. Active 1895-1920 on BLM land as part of the historic Birch Creek drainage.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - BLM Alaska Circle District

mines.name: Deadwood Creek Mine (Circle)
mines.description: Placer gold mine on Deadwood Creek in the Circle district east of Fairbanks near the Yukon River. Active during the pre-Klondike and early Alaska gold rush era on BLM land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - BLM Alaska Circle District

mines.name: Mammoth Creek Mine (Circle)
mines.description: Placer gold mine on Mammoth Creek in the Circle district associated with the early Yukon-area gold rush. Named for mammoth fossils in frozen placer gravels on BLM land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - BLM Alaska Circle District

mines.name: Ketchem Creek Mine
mines.description: Early Alaskan gold mine on Ketchem Creek in the Circle district part of the Birch Creek drainage system active from the mid-1890s on BLM land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - BLM Alaska Circle District

mines.name: Faith Creek Mine
mines.description: Placer gold mine on Faith Creek in the Circle district east of Fairbanks active 1896-1915 on BLM land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - BLM Alaska Circle District

mines.name: Independence Creek Mine (Circle)
mines.description: Placer gold mine on Independence Creek in the Circle mining district active during the late 1890s interior Alaska rush on BLM land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - BLM Alaska Circle District

mines.name: Eagle Creek Mine (Circle)
mines.description: Placer gold mine on Eagle Creek in the Circle district active during the interior Alaska gold rush era on BLM land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - BLM Alaska Circle District

mines.name: Portage Creek Mine (Circle)
mines.description: Placer gold mine on Portage Creek in the Circle district near the Yukon River active late 1890s-1910s on BLM land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - BLM Alaska Circle District

mines.name: Stampede Mine (Kantishna)
mines.description: Antimony and gold mine in the Kantishna district of Denali National Park the largest antimony producer in Alaska history. Mine buildings and ore processing equipment remain within Denali National Park and Preserve.

mines.name: Quigley Ridge Mine
mines.description: Gold and silver lode mine in the Kantishna Hills within Denali National Park part of the 1905 Kantishna gold rush on National Park Service land.

mines.name: Caribou Creek Mine (Kantishna)
mines.description: Placer and lode gold mine on Caribou Creek in the Kantishna Hills Denali National Park active during the 1905-1920 Kantishna boom on NPS public land.

mines.name: Eureka Mine (Kantishna)
mines.description: Gold-silver mine in the Kantishna Hills of Denali National Park one of several small operations during the 1905-1920 Kantishna rush. NPS land; some structures remain.

mines.name: Little Moose Creek Mine (Kantishna)
mines.description: Placer gold mine on Little Moose Creek in the Kantishna district within Denali National Park active during the brief 1905 Kantishna gold rush on NPS land.

mines.name: Quincy Mine
mines.description: One of the most famous copper mines in the Keweenaw Peninsula producing copper 1848-1945. The Quincy shaft house and hoist are preserved as a National Historic Landmark with public tours as part of Keweenaw National Historical Park.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - Keweenaw National Historical Park

mines.name: Calumet and Hecla Mine
mines.description: The legendary Calumet and Hecla Mining Company dominated world copper production 1870s-1910s extracting the Calumet Conglomerate lode producing over 5 billion pounds of copper. Shaft houses and industrial complex accessible within Keweenaw National Historical Park.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - Keweenaw National Historical Park

mines.name: Cliff Mine (Keweenaw)
mines.description: First commercially successful copper mine in the Lake Superior region opened 1845. The Cliff Mine pioneered deep shaft mining in Michigan copper country producing rich native copper masses. Historic ruins on Keweenaw National Historical Park land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - Keweenaw National Historical Park

mines.name: Central Mine (Keweenaw)
mines.description: Productive copper mine and company town on the Keweenaw Peninsula north of Phoenix. The Central Mine Company operated 1854-1898 producing over 50 million pounds of copper. Historic company town preserved with original structures on public land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - Keweenaw National Historical Park

mines.name: Delaware Mine (Keweenaw)
mines.description: Historic copper mine in the northern Keweenaw open for public tours. The Delaware Mine offers guided tours of original shafts and drifts with artifacts from the 1847-1887 operation. Tourist access preserved.

mines.name: Minesota Mine (Keweenaw)
mines.description: One of the earliest and richest native copper mines on the Keweenaw Peninsula near Rockland Michigan. The Minesota Mine produced massive native copper specimens weighing hundreds of tons operated 1847-1870. Michigan DNR public land.

mines.name: Adventure Mine
mines.description: Historic copper mine near Greenland Michigan open for public tours. The Adventure Mine complex preserves 19th century mining equipment and offers underground tours. Operated 1850-1920.

mines.name: Osceola Mine (Keweenaw)
mines.description: Important copper mine in the Calumet district worked the Osceola amygdaloid lode 1873-1930. Keweenaw National Historical Park land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - Keweenaw National Historical Park

mines.name: Tamarack Mine (Keweenaw)
mines.description: Deep copper mine in the Calumet district reaching exceptional depths for the era. The Tamarack Mining Company operated 1882-1930 working the Calumet and Hecla conglomerate at depth. Keweenaw NHP land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - Keweenaw National Historical Park

mines.name: Ahmeek Mine
mines.description: Copper mine in the northern Calumet district worked 1904-1930. The Ahmeek Mining Company operated as one of the last major Keweenaw mines before the district declined. Keweenaw NHP land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - Keweenaw National Historical Park

mines.name: Mohawk Mine (Keweenaw)
mines.description: Copper mine in the northern Calumet district near Mohawk Michigan worked the Keweenaw Mohawk lode 1898-1935. Keweenaw NHP land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - Keweenaw National Historical Park

mines.name: Trimountain Mine (Keweenaw)
mines.description: Copper mine in the Portage Lake district near Painesdale Michigan. The Trimountain Mining Company operated 1899-1935. The Trimountain manager's house is a listed historic structure on Keweenaw NHP land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - Keweenaw National Historical Park

mines.name: Copper Falls Mine
mines.description: Early copper mine and namesake of the Copper Falls fissure vein worked in the very earliest years of the Michigan copper rush 1845-1860s. Located near Eagle Harbor on National Forest land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - USFS Ottawa National Forest

mines.name: Eagle Harbor Mine
mines.description: Early copper mine near Eagle Harbor at the north end of the Keweenaw Peninsula among the first mines opened during the 1844-1845 copper rush. Located on Keweenaw National Historical Park associated land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - Keweenaw National Historical Park

mines.name: Ontonagon Boulder Site
mines.description: Site where the famous 3700-pound Ontonagon Boulder native copper mass was removed in 1843 setting off the Michigan copper rush. Located on the Ontonagon River on US Forest Service Ottawa National Forest land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - USFS Ottawa National Forest

mines.name: Kearsarge Mine (Keweenaw)
mines.description: Copper mine in the Calumet district worked the Keweenaw-Kearsarge lode 1880-1930. Part of the dense cluster of productive mines in the northern Keweenaw Peninsula on Keweenaw NHP land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - Keweenaw National Historical Park

mines.name: Wolverine Mine (Keweenaw)
mines.description: Copper mine in the northern Calumet district near Mohawk part of the Wolverine-Kearsarge lode system worked 1902-1920. Keweenaw NHP land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - Keweenaw National Historical Park

mines.name: Pewabic Mine (Keweenaw)
mines.description: Important copper mine in the Keweenaw near Hancock Michigan part of the Portage Lake copper lode district. Operated 1857-1920 producing significant native copper. Keweenaw NHP land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - Keweenaw National Historical Park

mines.name: Hancock Mine
mines.description: Copper mine in the Portage Lake district beneath the city of Hancock Michigan. Part of the central Keweenaw copper system worked 1861-1900. Keweenaw NHP land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - Keweenaw National Historical Park

mines.name: Franklin Mine (Keweenaw)
mines.description: Copper mine near Hancock Michigan in the Portage Lake district adjacent to the Quincy Mine. Operated in the latter half of the 19th century. Part of Keweenaw National Historical Park landscape.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - Keweenaw National Historical Park

mines.name: Atlantic Mine (Keweenaw)
mines.description: Copper mine in the Portage Lake district near Atlantic Mine Michigan. Part of the Baltic-Adventure lode system operated 1860-1915 on land within Keweenaw National Historical Park.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - Keweenaw National Historical Park

mines.name: Baltic Mine (Keweenaw)
mines.description: Productive copper mine in the Portage Lake district near Baltic Michigan. Part of the Baltic lode worked extensively 1865-1920. Located on Keweenaw National Historical Park associated land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - Keweenaw National Historical Park

mines.name: Champion Mine (Keweenaw)
mines.description: Copper mine in the Champion lode district of the Keweenaw Peninsula worked 1867-1910 producing native copper. Located on USFS Ottawa National Forest land in Houghton County.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - USFS Ottawa National Forest

mines.name: Isle Royale Mine (Keweenaw)
mines.description: Copper mine on the Keweenaw Peninsula named after the Isle Royale copper lode fissure system. Worked 1852-1875 in the northern Keweenaw on National Forest land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - USFS Ottawa National Forest

mines.name: Arcadian Mine (Keweenaw)
mines.description: Copper mine in the southern Portage Lake district near Painesdale Michigan. Part of the Arcadian lode system worked 1857-1910 on Keweenaw NHP associated land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - Keweenaw National Historical Park

mines.name: Jackson Iron Mine
mines.description: First iron mine in the Lake Superior region opened 1847 at the Jackson Iron Company location in Negaunee Michigan. Discovery of the Marquette Iron Range that became one of the most productive iron ranges in North America. Michigan DNR public land.

mines.name: Chapin Mine (Iron Mountain)
mines.description: Major iron ore mine in the Menominee Iron Range at Iron Mountain Michigan. The Chapin Mine required the world's largest steam pump. Operated 1879-1934; pump house preserved as Iron Mountain Iron Mine tourist attraction.

mines.name: Bessemer Mine (Gogebic Range)
mines.description: Iron ore mine near Bessemer Michigan in the Gogebic Iron Range along the Wisconsin border. The Gogebic Range was discovered in 1884 and rapidly developed. Ottawa National Forest land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - USFS Ottawa National Forest

mines.name: Ironwood Mine (Gogebic Range)
mines.description: Iron ore mine near Ironwood Michigan in the Gogebic Iron Range. Part of the last major Michigan iron range developed in the 1880s operated 1885-1920. Ottawa National Forest land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - USFS Ottawa National Forest

mines.name: Norrie Mine (Gogebic)
mines.description: Iron ore mine in the Gogebic Iron Range near Ironwood Michigan named for railroad executive Edwin Norrie who promoted the Gogebic district. Active 1886-1920. Ottawa National Forest land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - USFS Ottawa National Forest

mines.name: Sunday Lake Mine (Gogebic)
mines.description: Iron ore mine near Sunday Lake in the Gogebic Iron Range near Wakefield Michigan. Part of the extensive Gogebic Range mining district active 1887-1920. Ottawa National Forest land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - USFS Ottawa National Forest

mines.name: Republic Mine (Marquette Range)
mines.description: Iron ore mine in the Republic district of the Marquette Iron Range in Marquette County Michigan. Active 1870-1920s. Located on USFS Hiawatha National Forest land in the central Upper Peninsula.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - USFS Hiawatha National Forest

mines.name: Ishpeming Iron Range Mines
mines.description: Cluster of iron mines in and around Ishpeming Michigan in the heart of the Marquette Iron Range. Ishpeming was the center of Lake Superior iron mining from the 1840s-1920s with multiple mine shafts on public land.

mines.name: Iron Mountain Mine (Menominee)
mines.description: Iron ore mine in the Menominee Iron Range near Iron Mountain Michigan producing iron ore from the Chapin and Millie Mine system from 1879. Major producer through early 20th century. Michigan DNR land.

mines.name: Humboldt Iron Mine
mines.description: Early iron ore mine in the Marquette Iron Range near Humboldt Michigan. Active in the early development of the Lake Superior iron ore industry 1863-1900. Michigan DNR land.

mines.name: Norway Iron Mine (Menominee)
mines.description: Iron ore mine near Norway Michigan in the Menominee Iron Range. Part of the Norway-Iron Mountain mining district active 1877-1920. Michigan DNR public land.

mines.name: Crystal Falls Iron Mine
mines.description: Iron ore mine in the Iron River district of the Menominee Iron Range near Crystal Falls Michigan. Active 1880-1910. Ottawa National Forest/Michigan DNR land.

mines.name: Wakefield Mine (Gogebic)
mines.description: Iron ore mine in the Gogebic Iron Range near Wakefield Michigan. Part of the densely mined Gogebic trend active 1885-1925. Ottawa National Forest land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - USFS Ottawa National Forest

mines.name: Porcupine Mountains Mine
mines.description: Early copper and iron mine in the Porcupine Mountains area of the Upper Peninsula. Part of the Keweenaw-adjacent mineral district explored in the 1860s-1870s. Porcupine Mountains Wilderness State Park land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - Michigan DNR State Park

mines.name: Palmer Iron Mine (Marquette)
mines.description: Iron ore mine near Palmer Michigan in the Marquette Iron Range. Part of the extensive Marquette Range mining district active 1882-1920. Michigan DNR public land.

mines.name: Negaunee Iron Mine
mines.description: One of the earliest iron ore mines in the Marquette Iron Range at Negaunee Michigan adjacent to the Jackson discovery. Active 1847-1900. Michigan DNR public land.

mines.name: Millie Mine (Iron Mountain)
mines.description: Iron ore mine adjacent to the Chapin Mine at Iron Mountain Michigan in the Menominee Iron Range. Part of the Iron Mountain iron mining complex operated 1880-1915. Michigan DNR land.

mines.name: Iron River Mine (Menominee)
mines.description: Iron ore mine in the Iron River district of the Menominee Iron Range. Part of the western Upper Peninsula iron mining district active 1885-1920. Ottawa National Forest land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - USFS Ottawa National Forest

mines.name: Phoenix Mine (Keweenaw)
mines.description: Early Keweenaw copper mine near the community of Phoenix Michigan one of the first mines opened in the Michigan copper rush era 1843-1870s. Located on National Forest land in the northern Keweenaw.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - USFS Ottawa National Forest

mines.name: Mass Mine (Keweenaw)
mines.description: Early copper mine near Mass City Michigan in the Ontonagon County copper district. Part of the Minesota Mine group worked 1847-1870s. Ottawa National Forest land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - USFS Ottawa National Forest

mines.name: Norwich Mine (Keweenaw)
mines.description: Early copper mine in the Keweenaw between Eagle Harbor and Central mine districts. Worked in the early Michigan copper rush era 1849-1870. National Forest land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - USFS Ottawa National Forest

mines.name: Seneca Mine (Keweenaw)
mines.description: Copper mine in the Portage Lake district worked the Seneca lode 1871-1910 on Keweenaw NHP associated land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - Keweenaw National Historical Park

mines.name: Allouez Mine (Keweenaw)
mines.description: Copper mine in the Portage Lake district near Houghton Michigan. Part of the dense cluster of productive mines in the central Keweenaw Peninsula operated 1858-1910. Keweenaw NHP area.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - Keweenaw National Historical Park

mines.name: Centennial Mine (Keweenaw)
mines.description: Copper mine in the northern Calumet district opened 1875. Operated through the late 19th century on Keweenaw National Historical Park land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - Keweenaw National Historical Park

mines.name: Huron Mine (Keweenaw)
mines.description: Copper mine in the Portage Lake district near Houghton and Hancock Michigan. Part of the mid-Keweenaw copper lode system operated 1860-1900. Keweenaw NHP associated land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - Keweenaw National Historical Park

mines.name: LaSalle Mine (Keweenaw)
mines.description: Copper mine in the Portage Lake district worked during the height of the Michigan copper boom 1862-1905. Keweenaw NHP associated land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - Keweenaw National Historical Park

mines.name: South Lake Linden Mine
mines.description: Copper mine in the Calumet district near Lake Linden Michigan on Torch Lake. Part of the extensive Calumet conglomerate lode system worked 1875-1915. Keweenaw NHP land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - Keweenaw National Historical Park

mines.name: Eckley Miners Village Mine
mines.description: Anthracite coal mine at the preserved company town of Eckley in Luzerne County Pennsylvania. The mine and company village date from 1854 and are preserved as Eckley Miners Village State Historic Site offering public tours of the intact mining landscape.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - Pennsylvania State Historic Site

mines.name: Lehigh Coal Mine (Mauch Chunk)
mines.description: Historic anthracite coal mine at Mauch Chunk now Jim Thorpe Pennsylvania one of the earliest coal mines in the region. The Lehigh Coal and Navigation Company developed this area in the 1820s. Pennsylvania state park and public historic site.

mines.name: Mammoth Vein Mine (Pottsville)
mines.description: Major anthracite coal mine working the Mammoth Vein the thickest coal seam in the Schuylkill coal field near Pottsville Pennsylvania. Active 1840-1900. Public historic site in Schuylkill County.

mines.name: Pottsville Coal Mines
mines.description: Cluster of early anthracite coal mines surrounding Pottsville Pennsylvania the commercial capital of the Schuylkill coal field. Active 1830-1890. Pottsville was the first city chartered in Pennsylvania coal country.

mines.name: No. 9 Mine and Museum (Lansford)
mines.description: Preserved anthracite coal mine in Lansford Pennsylvania open for public tours. The No. 9 Mine worked from 1855 until 1972 and is now a museum with underground tours of original workings. Carbon County public heritage site.

mines.name: Scranton Anthracite Mine
mines.description: Historic anthracite coal mine in the Lackawanna Valley at Scranton Pennsylvania. Part of the Wyoming/Lackawanna coal field worked from the 1840s. The Lackawanna Coal Mine Tour preserves original workings for public access.

mines.name: Wilkes-Barre Coal Mines
mines.description: Historic anthracite coal mines surrounding Wilkes-Barre Pennsylvania in the Wyoming Valley coal field. The Wyoming Valley was one of the earliest and most productive anthracite regions. Active 1830-1900. Luzerne County public historic sites.

mines.name: Centralia Coal Mine
mines.description: Anthracite coal mine at the now-famous abandoned town of Centralia Columbia County Pennsylvania. Mine and town operated 1856-1962 until an underground mine fire rendered the town uninhabitable. Public access to abandoned townsite on state land.

mines.name: Hazelton Coal Mine
mines.description: Anthracite coal mine at Hazleton Pennsylvania in the Hazleton Basin of the northern anthracite field. Part of the productive Black Creek coal district active 1858-1900. Luzerne/Carbon County public land.

mines.name: Shenandoah Valley Mine (PA)
mines.description: Anthracite coal mine in the Shenandoah Valley of Schuylkill County Pennsylvania. The Shenandoah area was one of the most intensively mined portions of the middle anthracite field active 1862-1920.

mines.name: Ashland Coal Mine (PA)
mines.description: Anthracite coal mine at Ashland in the northern Schuylkill County coal field Pennsylvania. Part of the Middle Anthracite Coal Field worked 1855-1910. Public historic site in Schuylkill County.

mines.name: Girardville Mine (PA)
mines.description: Anthracite coal mine at Girardville in the Schuylkill County coal field Pennsylvania. Part of the eastern middle anthracite field worked from the 1850s through the early 20th century.

mines.name: Mount Carmel Mine (PA)
mines.description: Anthracite coal mine at Mount Carmel in Northumberland County Pennsylvania in the western middle anthracite field. Active 1852-1910. Part of the extensive Pennsylvania coal region.

mines.name: Shamokin Coal Mine (PA)
mines.description: Anthracite coal mine at Shamokin in Northumberland County Pennsylvania. The Shamokin area was extensively mined from the 1840s producing anthracite coal from multiple veins. Public historic area.

mines.name: Carbondale Mine (PA)
mines.description: One of the earliest anthracite mines in the Lackawanna Valley at Carbondale Pennsylvania. Carbondale was the site of the first anthracite coal operations in the Wyoming-Lackawanna field begun in the late 1820s. Historic public site.

mines.name: Plymouth Mine (PA)
mines.description: Anthracite coal mine near Plymouth in Luzerne County Pennsylvania in the Wyoming Valley field. Part of the extensive Wyoming field worked from the 1840s producing high-quality anthracite.

mines.name: Pittston Mine (PA)
mines.description: Anthracite coal mine at Pittston in Luzerne County Pennsylvania in the Wyoming Valley anthracite field. Active 1838-1900. The region was intensively mined in the 19th century and is now a public historical area.

mines.name: Nanticoke Mine (PA)
mines.description: Anthracite coal mine at Nanticoke in the Wyoming Valley field Luzerne County Pennsylvania. Part of the extensive Wyoming Valley coal field worked from the 1840s. Public historic area.

mines.name: Duryea Mine (PA)
mines.description: Anthracite coal mine at Duryea in Luzerne County Pennsylvania in the Lackawanna Valley coal field. Active 1860-1910. Part of the northeastern Pennsylvania anthracite district.

mines.name: Olyphant Mine (PA)
mines.description: Anthracite coal mine at Olyphant in Lackawanna County Pennsylvania in the lower Lackawanna Valley coal field. Active 1862-1910. Part of the Scranton-area anthracite district.

mines.name: Jermyn Mine (PA)
mines.description: Anthracite coal mine at Jermyn in Lackawanna County Pennsylvania. Part of the northern anthracite field worked in the post-Civil War era active 1865-1910.

mines.name: Peckville Mine (PA)
mines.description: Anthracite coal mine at Peckville Blakely Township in Lackawanna County Pennsylvania. Part of the Lackawanna Valley coal field active 1868-1910.

mines.name: Forest City Mine (PA)
mines.description: Anthracite coal mine at Forest City in the northern Lackawanna County coal field Pennsylvania. Part of the northern terminus of the anthracite region active 1870-1910.

mines.name: St. Clair Mine (PA)
mines.description: Anthracite coal mine at St. Clair in the Pottsville area of the eastern middle anthracite field Pennsylvania. Part of the early development of the Schuylkill coal field active 1845-1895.

mines.name: New Philadelphia Mine (PA)
mines.description: Anthracite coal mine at New Philadelphia in Schuylkill County Pennsylvania. Part of the eastern middle anthracite field worked from the late 1840s through the turn of the 20th century.

mines.name: Port Carbon Mine (PA)
mines.description: Anthracite coal mine and early shipping point at Port Carbon in Schuylkill County Pennsylvania on the Schuylkill Canal. One of the earliest coal shipping centers active from 1832.

mines.name: Tamaqua Mine (PA)
mines.description: Anthracite coal mine at Tamaqua in the upper Schuylkill County coal field Pennsylvania. One of the early coal mining communities active from the 1830s. Part of the extensive northern anthracite field.

mines.name: Coaldale Mine (PA)
mines.description: Anthracite coal mine at Coaldale in Carbon County Pennsylvania in the upper Lehigh coal field. Active 1850-1900. Part of the productive Lehigh coal field district.

mines.name: Summit Hill Mine (PA)
mines.description: One of the earliest anthracite coal mines in Carbon County Pennsylvania at Summit Hill. The Lehigh Coal and Navigation Company began operations at Summit Hill in the early 1820s making it among the first organized coal operations in the region.

mines.name: Nesquehoning Mine (PA)
mines.description: Anthracite coal mine at Nesquehoning in Carbon County Pennsylvania in the Lehigh coal field. Part of the early Lehigh coal district active 1838-1900.

mines.name: Weatherly Mine (PA)
mines.description: Anthracite coal mine near Weatherly in Carbon County Pennsylvania in the northern Lehigh coal field. Part of the extended Lehigh-Schuylkill anthracite region active 1845-1900.

mines.name: Drifton Mine (PA)
mines.description: Anthracite coal mine at Drifton in Luzerne County Pennsylvania worked by Eckley B. Coxe the mine engineer and philanthropist. The Drifton Mine was a well-organized operation 1855-1895. Public historic area.

mines.name: Freeland Mine (PA)
mines.description: Anthracite coal mine at Freeland in Luzerne County Pennsylvania in the Black Creek section of the northern anthracite field. Active 1868-1910.

mines.name: Beaver Meadows Mine (PA)
mines.description: Early anthracite coal mine at Beaver Meadows in Carbon County Pennsylvania. One of the early developments in the Lehigh coal field active from the late 1830s.

mines.name: Audenried Mine (PA)
mines.description: Anthracite coal mine at Audenried in Luzerne County Pennsylvania in the northern Black Creek section of the anthracite field. Active 1862-1905.

mines.name: McAdoo Mine (PA)
mines.description: Anthracite coal mine at McAdoo in Schuylkill and Luzerne Counties Pennsylvania. Part of the northern anthracite field active 1865-1910.

mines.name: Delano Mine (PA)
mines.description: Anthracite coal mine at Delano in Schuylkill County Pennsylvania in the upper Mahanoy Valley coal field. Part of the northern portion of the middle anthracite field active 1870-1910.

mines.name: Mahanoy City Mine (PA)
mines.description: Anthracite coal mine near Mahanoy City in Schuylkill County Pennsylvania in the Mahanoy Valley coal field. One of the larger towns in the middle anthracite region active 1855-1910.

mines.name: Monte Cristo Mine (WA)
mines.description: One of the richest silver-gold mines in Washington State at the Monte Cristo townsite in the Cascade Mountains. The Monte Cristo mines produced millions in silver-gold ore 1893-1907. Historic townsite and mine ruins on Mt. Baker-Snoqualmie National Forest land accessible via Sauk River trail.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - Mt. Baker-Snoqualmie National Forest

mines.name: Rainier Mine (Monte Cristo)
mines.description: Gold-silver mine in the Monte Cristo district of the north Cascades. Part of the cluster of mines that made Monte Cristo the most famous mining town in Washington State. Located on Mt. Baker-Snoqualmie National Forest land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - Mt. Baker-Snoqualmie National Forest

mines.name: Seventy-Six Mine (Monte Cristo)
mines.description: Silver-lead mine in the Monte Cristo district of Snohomish County Washington. Active during the 1890s Monte Cristo boom on National Forest land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - Mt. Baker-Snoqualmie National Forest

mines.name: Pride of the Mountains Mine (Monte Cristo)
mines.description: Gold-silver mine in the Monte Cristo mining district Snohomish County Washington. Active during the 1890s boom on Mt. Baker-Snoqualmie National Forest land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - Mt. Baker-Snoqualmie National Forest

mines.name: Index Gold Mine (WA)
mines.description: Gold-silver mine near Index Washington in the Skykomish River valley Cascade Mountains. The Index mining district produced gold 1896-1920. Located on Mt. Baker-Snoqualmie National Forest land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - Mt. Baker-Snoqualmie National Forest

mines.name: Mineral City Mine (WA)
mines.description: Gold and silver mine at Mineral City in the upper Silver Creek drainage near Index Washington. The Mineral City district was active 1890-1920 on National Forest land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - Mt. Baker-Snoqualmie National Forest

mines.name: Liberty Gold Mine (WA)
mines.description: Gold mine near Liberty Washington in the Swauk Creek drainage east of the Cascades. Liberty ghost town was Washington's first gold rush in 1873. Multiple mines on Okanogan-Wenatchee National Forest land accessible from the Liberty recreation area.

mines.name: Swauk Creek Mine (WA)
mines.description: Placer gold mine on Swauk Creek in the Liberty/Blewett district of Kittitas County Washington. The Swauk Creek drainage has been actively mined since the 1873 gold rush on Okanogan-Wenatchee National Forest land.

mines.name: Blewett Mine (WA)
mines.description: Gold mine at Blewett townsite in the Peshastin Creek drainage of Chelan County Washington. Active 1874-1920. Blewett was a noted gold mining community. Located on Okanogan-Wenatchee National Forest land.

mines.name: Republic Mine (WA)
mines.description: Major gold mine near Republic Washington in the Eureka mining district of Ferry County. The Republic area produced substantial gold and silver 1896-1930. Multiple mines on Colville National Forest land.

mines.name: Quilomene Mine (WA)
mines.description: Gold mine in the Quilomene area of eastern Kittitas County Washington. Active during the late 1890s east Cascades gold rush on BLM land.

mines.name: Holden Mine (WA)
mines.description: Major copper-zinc-gold mine at Holden Village in Chelan County Washington producing 212 million pounds of copper and 2.8 million ounces of gold 1938-1957. The historic mine site is now part of the Wenatchee National Forest with public access to Holden Village.

mines.name: Conconully Silver Mine (WA)
mines.description: Silver mine near the ghost town of Conconully Washington in Okanogan County. Conconully was an 1880s silver boom town. Multiple mines on Okanogan-Wenatchee National Forest and BLM land.

mines.name: Chelan Falls Mine (WA)
mines.description: Gold mine in the Chelan Falls area of Chelan County Washington. Part of the eastern Washington gold prospecting era active 1897-1920 on BLM/State land.

mines.name: Slate Creek Mine (WA)
mines.description: Gold placer mine on Slate Creek in the upper Skagit River drainage of northern Washington. Active 1878-1920 on Okanogan-Wenatchee National Forest land. One of the early gold discoveries in the North Cascades.

mines.name: Boundary Red Mountain Mine (WA)
mines.description: Gold-silver mine near the Canadian border in Ferry County Washington on Red Mountain. Active during the late 1890s gold rush era on Colville National Forest land.

mines.name: Orient Mine (WA)
mines.description: Gold mine near Orient Washington in Ferry County. Part of the eastern Washington gold boom era 1899-1920 on Colville National Forest land.

mines.name: Bodie Mine (WA)
mines.description: Gold mine near the ghost town of Bodie Washington in Okanogan County. Active 1897-1920. Multiple mines in the area on Colville National Forest land near the Canadian border.

mines.name: Eureka Mine (Republic WA)
mines.description: Gold mine in the Eureka district adjacent to Republic Washington. Part of the productive Ferry County gold fields active 1896-1925 on Colville National Forest land.

mines.name: Pride Mine (Republic WA)
mines.description: Gold-silver mine in the Republic district of Ferry County Washington. Part of the productive Republic mining area active 1898-1920 on Colville National Forest land.

mines.name: Reco Mine (Republic WA)
mines.description: Gold mine in the Republic district of Ferry County Washington. Active during the early 20th century Republic gold rush on Colville National Forest land.

mines.name: Kettle Falls Mine (WA)
mines.description: Gold mine in the Kettle Falls area of Stevens County Washington. Part of the eastern Washington gold prospecting era active 1896-1915 on Colville National Forest land.

mines.name: Eureka Mine (Republic WA) No. 2
mines.description: Secondary gold mine in the Eureka-Republic district of Ferry County Washington. Active 1897-1920 on Colville National Forest public land.

mines.name: Steamboat Mine (WA)
mines.description: Gold mine in northern Ferry County Washington near the Canadian border. Active during the late 1890s gold boom on Colville National Forest land.

mines.name: Jacksonville Gold Mine (OR)
mines.description: Placer gold mine at Jacksonville Oregon site of the 1852 gold discovery that founded the town. Jacksonville is a National Historic Landmark preserving the complete gold rush townscape. Multiple mine sites in the Applegate district on BLM land.

mines.name: Applegate River Mine (OR)
mines.description: Gold placer mine along the Applegate River in the Applegate Valley of Jackson County Oregon. Rich placer gold produced continuously from 1853 through the early 20th century. BLM land in the Rogue River-Siskiyou National Forest area.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - BLM Oregon / Rogue River-Siskiyou NF

mines.name: Buncom Mine (OR)
mines.description: Gold mine at the ghost town of Buncom in the upper Applegate Valley of Jackson County Oregon. Buncom was a thriving gold mining community 1855-1905. Multiple mine ruins on BLM land in the Applegate drainage.

mines.name: Sterling Mine (OR)
mines.description: Hydraulic gold mine in the Bunker Hill area of Jackson County Oregon. The Sterling Mine used one of the longest ditch systems in Oregon to supply hydraulic water. Active 1877-1905 on Rogue River-Siskiyou National Forest land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - Rogue River-Siskiyou National Forest

mines.name: Sumpter Mine (OR)
mines.description: Gold mine at Sumpter Oregon the center of the Baker County gold rush. The Sumpter Valley gold fields produced millions in gold 1862-1940. The historic Sumpter Dredge is preserved as a state park open to visitors on public land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - Oregon State Parks / BLM Oregon

mines.name: Sumpter Valley Dredge Site
mines.description: Gold dredge operation in the Sumpter Valley Baker County Oregon. Three dredges worked the Powder River gravels 1912-1954. The No. 3 dredge is preserved as Sumpter Valley Dredge State Heritage Area open for public tours.

mines.name: Baker City Gold Mines (OR)
mines.description: Gold mines surrounding Baker City Oregon center of the Blue Mountains gold rush. The Baker area produced over 5 million ounces of gold. Multiple mine sites on BLM and Wallowa-Whitman National Forest land surrounding Baker City.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - BLM Oregon / Wallowa-Whitman NF

mines.name: Unity Mine (OR)
mines.description: Gold mine near Unity in the Burnt River drainage of Baker County Oregon. Part of the extended Blue Mountains gold district active 1870-1920. BLM and Wallowa-Whitman National Forest land.

mines.name: Cornucopia Mine (OR)
mines.description: Rich gold-silver mine at the Cornucopia ghost town in the Wallowa Mountains of Baker County Oregon. Produced millions in gold 1884-1942. Located on Wallowa-Whitman National Forest land; ghost town ruins remain accessible.

mines.name: Granite Mine (OR)
mines.description: Gold mine at the ghost town of Granite in Grant County Oregon. Granite was a thriving placer gold camp from 1862 and later a lode mining center. Multiple mine sites on Malheur National Forest land.

mines.name: Bohemia Mine (OR)
mines.description: Gold-silver mine in the Bohemia district of Lane County Oregon in the Calapooya Mountains. The Bohemia district was discovered in 1863 by James 'Bohemia' Johnson. Multiple mines on Umpqua National Forest land accessible from Cottage Grove.

mines.name: Champion Mine (Bohemia OR)
mines.description: Gold mine in the Bohemia district of Lane County Oregon one of the most productive mines in the district. Active 1890-1920 on Umpqua National Forest land.

mines.name: Musick Mine (Bohemia OR)
mines.description: Gold mine in the Bohemia mining district Lane County Oregon. The Musick Mine was one of the higher-producing operations in the Bohemia district active 1895-1920. Umpqua National Forest land.

mines.name: Galice Mine (OR)
mines.description: Gold mine near Galice Oregon on the Rogue River in Josephine County. The Galice area has been mined since the 1850s gold rush. Located on BLM land along the Rogue River with the Illinois Valley placer fields nearby.

mines.name: Waldo Placer Mine (OR)
mines.description: Gold placer mine at Waldo in the Illinois Valley of Josephine County Oregon. Waldo was an early gold camp in the Klamath Mountains active 1852-1900. BLM land in the Siskiyou Mountains.

mines.name: Kerby Mine (OR)
mines.description: Gold mine near Kerby formerly known as Kerbyville in Josephine County Oregon. Part of the Illinois Valley gold district active 1854-1900 on BLM land.

mines.name: Gold Beach Mine (OR)
mines.description: Placer gold mine on the beach sands near Gold Beach at the mouth of the Rogue River in Curry County Oregon. Beach gold discovered 1853 gave the town its name. Oregon coast state beaches are public land.

mines.name: Myrtle Creek Mine (OR)
mines.description: Gold and nickel mine near Myrtle Creek in Douglas County Oregon. Part of the extended Applegate-Umpqua mineral district active 1856-1910 on BLM land.

mines.name: Canyon City Mine (OR)
mines.description: Placer gold mine at Canyon City the seat of Grant County Oregon. The John Day River gold rush of 1862 brought 10,000 miners to the area. Canyon City has been continuously inhabited since. Multiple mine sites on BLM land.

mines.name: John Day Mine (OR)
mines.description: Placer gold mine on John Day River in Grant County Oregon. Part of the 1862 Blue Mountains gold rush that brought thousands of miners. BLM land along the John Day River corridor.

mines.name: Susanville Mine (OR)
mines.description: Gold mine near Susanville in the Silvies Valley area of Grant County Oregon. Part of the Blue Mountains gold district active 1870-1910. Malheur National Forest land.

mines.name: Bourne Mine (OR)
mines.description: Gold mine at Bourne ghost town in the Cracker Creek drainage of Baker County Oregon. Bourne was a thriving gold camp in the 1880s-1900s. Mine ruins on Wallowa-Whitman National Forest land.

mines.name: Sparta Mine (OR)
mines.description: Hydraulic gold mine at Sparta ghost town in Baker County Oregon. Sparta was one of the richest hydraulic gold mines in eastern Oregon active 1870-1910. Wallowa-Whitman National Forest land.

mines.name: Homestead Mine (OR)
mines.description: Gold-copper mine near Homestead Oregon in the deep Hells Canyon area of Baker County. Active 1899-1920. Wallowa-Whitman National Forest land in the Snake River breaks.

mines.name: Moose Creek Mine (Fairbanks)
mines.description: Placer gold mine on Moose Creek near the Fairbanks district active in the early 20th century on BLM land.

mines.name: Globe Creek Mine (Fairbanks)
mines.description: Placer gold mine on Globe Creek in the northern Fairbanks mining district worked in the early 20th century on BLM land.

mines.name: Emma Creek Mine (Fairbanks)
mines.description: Placer gold mine on Emma Creek in the Fairbanks mining district active during the 1900s boom on BLM land.

mines.name: Eva Creek Mine (Fairbanks)
mines.description: Placer gold mine on Eva Creek west of the main Fairbanks district with intermittent operation on BLM/State land.

mines.name: O'Connor Creek Mine
mines.description: Placer gold mine on O'Connor Creek in the eastern Fairbanks district active in the early 20th century on BLM land.

mines.name: Independence Creek Mine (Fairbanks)
mines.description: Placer gold mine on Independence Creek west of the main Fairbanks district active in the early 20th century on BLM land.

mines.name: Montana Creek Mine (Fairbanks)
mines.description: Placer gold mine on Montana Creek in the western Fairbanks district active during the early 20th century boom on BLM land.

mines.name: Woodchopper Creek Mine
mines.description: Placer gold mine on Woodchopper Creek in the Yukon drainage near Circle AK part of the extended Yukon placer district on BLM land.

mines.name: Bear Creek Mine (Iditarod)
mines.description: Placer gold mine on Bear Creek a tributary of the Iditarod drainage near Flat active during the Iditarod rush era on BLM land.

mines.name: Willow Creek Mine (Iditarod)
mines.description: Placer gold mine on Willow Creek in the Iditarod district near Flat Alaska active 1909-1920s on BLM land.

mines.name: Yankee Creek Mine (Iditarod)
