mines.description: Placer gold mine on Treasure Creek in the eastern Fairbanks district. Active 1907-1920 on BLM Alaska Interior land.

mines.name: Caribou Creek Mine (Fairbanks)
mines.description: Placer gold mine on Caribou Creek west of the main Fairbanks district. Active early 20th century on BLM land.

mines.name: Quartz Creek Mine (Fairbanks)
mines.description: Placer gold mine on Quartz Creek in the eastern Fairbanks district. Active 1907-1930 on BLM Alaska Interior land.

mines.name: Birch Creek Mine (Fairbanks)
mines.description: Placer gold mine on Birch Creek south of Fairbanks in the Chena Ridge area. Active early 20th century on BLM/State land.

mines.name: Alder Creek Mine (Fairbanks)
mines.description: Placer gold mine on Alder Creek in the western Fairbanks district. Active 1908-1930 on BLM Alaska Interior land.

mines.name: Cliff No. 2 Mine (Keweenaw)
mines.description: Extension workings of the famous Cliff Mine in the northern Keweenaw. Worked alongside the main Cliff workings during the height of the copper boom 1846-1870 on National Forest land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - USFS Ottawa National Forest

mines.name: Eagle River Mine (Keweenaw)
mines.description: Copper mine and early townsite at Eagle River on the Keweenaw Peninsula. One of the earliest copper mining communities established 1843. National Forest and state land along Lake Superior shore.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - USFS Ottawa National Forest

mines.name: Mandan Mine (Keweenaw)
mines.description: Copper mine in the northern Keweenaw worked during the middle of the copper boom era 1857-1880. National Forest land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - USFS Ottawa National Forest

mines.name: Minnesota Mine (Keweenaw)
mines.description: Copper mine in the Calumet district Keweenaw Peninsula. Different property from the Minesota Mine. Active 1856-1900 on Keweenaw NHP associated land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - Keweenaw National Historical Park

mines.name: Quincy Mine No. 2 (Keweenaw)
mines.description: Secondary shaft and working of the famous Quincy Mine complex on the Keweenaw Peninsula. Part of Keweenaw National Historical Park with historic equipment and structures.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - Keweenaw National Historical Park

mines.name: Calumet Conglomerate Shaft
mines.description: One of the many shafts sunk to work the famous Calumet Conglomerate Lode the richest copper deposit in the Keweenaw. Located in the core of the C&H operations on Keweenaw NHP land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - Keweenaw National Historical Park

mines.name: Houghton Mine (Keweenaw)
mines.description: Copper mine in the Portage Lake district beneath Houghton Michigan named for Michigan geologist Douglass Houghton who first identified the copper deposits. Keweenaw NHP area.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - Keweenaw National Historical Park

mines.name: Calumet and Hecla Shaft 2
mines.description: Secondary shaft in the Calumet and Hecla mine system worked from 1872. Part of the world-famous C&H complex that produced 5 billion pounds of copper. Keweenaw NHP land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - Keweenaw National Historical Park

mines.name: Torch Lake Mine (Keweenaw)
mines.description: Copper mine on the east side of Torch Lake in the Calumet district. The Torch Lake area received copper stamp sands from multiple mills. Keweenaw NHP land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - Keweenaw National Historical Park

mines.name: South Range Mine (Keweenaw)
mines.description: Copper mine in the southern Portage Lake district near South Range Michigan. Active 1902-1925 on Keweenaw NHP and associated land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - Keweenaw National Historical Park

mines.name: Brownsville Mine (PA)
mines.description: Anthracite coal mine at Brownsville in Luzerne County Pennsylvania. Part of the northern anthracite field active 1858-1905.

mines.name: Barnesville Mine (PA)
mines.description: Anthracite coal mine at Barnesville in Schuylkill County Pennsylvania. Part of the middle anthracite field active 1863-1910.

mines.name: Nuremberg Mine (PA)
mines.description: Anthracite coal mine at Nuremberg in Luzerne County Pennsylvania near the Hazleton area. Active 1867-1910.

mines.name: Ebervale Mine (PA)
mines.description: Anthracite coal mine at Ebervale in Luzerne County Pennsylvania near Hazleton. Active 1872-1910.

mines.name: Harwood Mine (PA)
mines.description: Anthracite coal mine at Harwood in Luzerne County Pennsylvania. Part of the northern Black Creek coal district active 1875-1910.

mines.name: Jeddo Mine (PA)
mines.description: Anthracite coal mine at Jeddo in Luzerne County Pennsylvania near Hazleton. Active 1863-1910.

mines.name: Eckley Mine No. 2 (PA)
mines.description: Additional anthracite coal workings associated with the Eckley Miners Village in Luzerne County Pennsylvania. Part of the preserved company town mining complex.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - Pennsylvania State Historic Site

mines.name: Tomhicken Mine (PA)
mines.description: Anthracite coal mine at Tomhicken in Luzerne County Pennsylvania. Active 1869-1910 as part of the Black Creek coal field.

mines.name: Lattimer Mine (PA)
mines.description: Anthracite coal mine at Lattimer in Luzerne County Pennsylvania historically significant as the site of the 1897 Lattimer Massacre. State historic site and public land.

mines.name: Monte Cristo Mine No. 2 (WA)
mines.description: Additional silver-gold mine workings in the Monte Cristo district Snohomish County Washington. Active 1896-1910 on Mt. Baker-Snoqualmie National Forest land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - Mt. Baker-Snoqualmie National Forest

mines.name: Wilmans Mine (Monte Cristo WA)
mines.description: Gold-silver mine at Wilmans Peaks in the Monte Cristo district Snohomish County Washington. Active during the 1890s Monte Cristo boom on National Forest land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - Mt. Baker-Snoqualmie National Forest

mines.name: Comet Mine (Monte Cristo WA)
mines.description: Silver mine in the Monte Cristo district. Part of the productive silver-gold lode system that made Monte Cristo famous. Located on Mt. Baker-Snoqualmie National Forest land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - Mt. Baker-Snoqualmie National Forest

mines.name: Justice Mine (Monte Cristo WA)
mines.description: Gold-silver mine in the Monte Cristo district of Snohomish County Washington. Active during the Monte Cristo boom of the 1890s on National Forest land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - Mt. Baker-Snoqualmie National Forest

mines.name: Glacier Mine (Monte Cristo WA)
mines.description: Gold mine adjacent to Glacier Basin in the Monte Cristo district. Active 1895-1907 before the railroads ceased operation. Mt. Baker-Snoqualmie National Forest land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - Mt. Baker-Snoqualmie National Forest

mines.name: Peshastin Mine (WA)
mines.description: Gold placer mine on Peshastin Creek near Blewett in Chelan County Washington. Part of the earliest Washington gold rush district active 1873-1920 on Okanogan-Wenatchee National Forest land.

mines.name: Ingalls Creek Mine (WA)
mines.description: Gold mine on Ingalls Creek in Chelan County Washington. Active 1895-1915 on Okanogan-Wenatchee National Forest land near the Alpine Lakes Wilderness.

mines.name: Peshastin Creek Mine No. 2 (WA)
mines.description: Additional gold mine workings on Peshastin Creek near Blewett Chelan County Washington. Part of the early Washington gold district active 1878-1920 on National Forest land.

mines.name: Tronsen Creek Mine (WA)
mines.description: Gold placer mine on Tronsen Creek in Chelan County Washington. Active 1880-1915 on Okanogan-Wenatchee National Forest land.

mines.name: Mission Creek Mine (WA)
mines.description: Gold mine on Mission Creek in Chelan County Washington. Active 1893-1915 on Okanogan-Wenatchee National Forest land.

mines.name: Methow Valley Mine (WA)
mines.description: Gold mine in the Methow Valley of Okanogan County Washington. Part of the north Cascades mining district active 1895-1915 on Okanogan-Wenatchee National Forest land.

mines.name: Mazama Mine (WA)
mines.description: Gold mine near Mazama Washington in the upper Methow Valley. Active 1897-1918 on Okanogan-Wenatchee National Forest land.

mines.name: Winthrop Mine (WA)
mines.description: Gold mine near Winthrop Washington in the Methow Valley of Okanogan County. Active 1893-1915 on Okanogan-Wenatchee National Forest land.

mines.name: Twisp Mine (WA)
mines.description: Gold mine near Twisp in the Methow Valley of Okanogan County Washington. Active 1895-1915 on Okanogan-Wenatchee National Forest land.

mines.name: Pateros Mine (WA)
mines.description: Gold mine near Pateros at the confluence of the Methow and Columbia Rivers Okanogan County Washington. Active early 20th century on BLM land.

mines.name: Illinois River Mine (OR)
mines.description: Gold placer mine along the Illinois River in Josephine County Oregon one of the early gold discoveries in southwestern Oregon. Active 1852-1920 on BLM land.

mines.name: Slate Creek Mine (OR)
mines.description: Placer gold mine on Slate Creek a tributary of the Illinois River in Josephine County Oregon. Active 1854-1900 on BLM land.

mines.name: Sucker Creek Mine (OR)
mines.description: Placer gold mine on Sucker Creek in the Illinois Valley of Josephine County Oregon. Active 1853-1900 on BLM and National Forest land.

mines.name: East Fork Illinois Mine (OR)
mines.description: Gold mine on the East Fork of the Illinois River in Josephine County Oregon. Active 1855-1900 on BLM land in the Klamath Mountains.

mines.name: Josephine Mine (OR)
mines.description: Gold mine named for the county named for an early woman prospector. Located in the Applegate drainage of Jackson County Oregon. Active 1853-1900 on BLM land.

mines.name: Indian Creek Mine (OR Rogue)
mines.description: Placer gold mine on Indian Creek in the Rogue River drainage of Jackson County Oregon. Active 1855-1900 on BLM and Rogue River-Siskiyou National Forest land.

mines.name: Little Applegate Mine (OR)
mines.description: Gold mine on Little Applegate Creek in Jackson County Oregon. Active 1854-1900 on Rogue River-Siskiyou National Forest land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - Rogue River-Siskiyou National Forest

mines.name: Gin Lin Trail Mine (OR)
mines.description: Chinese hydraulic gold mining operation along the Little Applegate River. Gin Lin was a successful Chinese entrepreneur who ran hydraulic placer operations 1878-1894. Site on BLM land preserved as Gin Lin Trail hiking area.

mines.name: Orofino Mine (OR Baker)
mines.description: Early gold mine near Orofino Creek in Baker County Oregon. Part of the first wave of Blue Mountains gold discovery 1861-1862 on BLM land.

mines.name: Medical Springs Mine (OR)
mines.description: Gold mine near Medical Springs in Baker County Oregon in the Wallowa foothills. Active 1875-1910 on Wallowa-Whitman National Forest land.

mines.name: Rock Creek Mine (OR Grant)
mines.description: Placer gold mine on Rock Creek near Canyon City in Grant County Oregon. Active during the 1862-1880s Blue Mountains gold rush on BLM land.

mines.name: Whisky Creek Mine (OR Grant)
mines.description: Placer gold mine on Whisky Creek in the Canyon City area Grant County Oregon. Active during the early 1860s gold rush on BLM land.

mines.name: Prairie City Mine (OR)
mines.description: Gold mine near Prairie City in Grant County Oregon in the Blue Mountains. Active 1875-1910 on Malheur National Forest land.

mines.name: Logan Valley Mine (OR)
mines.description: Gold mine in Logan Valley of Harney County Oregon. Active 1878-1910 on BLM land in the high desert mountains.

mines.name: Camp Creek Mine (OR Grant)
mines.description: Placer gold mine on Camp Creek in the John Day area of Grant County Oregon. Active during the 1860s Blue Mountains rush on BLM land.

mines.name: Dayville Mine (OR)
mines.description: Gold mine near Dayville in Grant County Oregon in the John Day Valley. Active 1870-1910 on BLM land.

mines.name: Trap Rock Mine (Keweenaw)
mines.description: Early copper mine in the northern Keweenaw Peninsula working trap rock copper veins near the Cliff Mine area. Active 1850-1870 on Ottawa National Forest land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - USFS Ottawa National Forest

mines.name: Grant Mine (Keweenaw)
mines.description: Copper mine in the Portage Lake district of the Keweenaw named for Ulysses S. Grant. Active during the post-Civil War copper boom 1866-1895 on Keweenaw NHP associated land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - Keweenaw National Historical Park

mines.name: Stanton Mine (Keweenaw)
mines.description: Copper mine in the Portage Lake district worked in the late 19th century boom. Located on Keweenaw National Historical Park associated land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - Keweenaw National Historical Park

mines.name: Caledonia Mine (Keweenaw)
mines.description: Copper mine in the Portage Lake district of the Keweenaw active during the Civil War era copper boom. Keweenaw NHP area.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - Keweenaw National Historical Park

mines.name: Portage Lake Mine (Keweenaw)
mines.description: Copper mine in the namesake Portage Lake district of the Keweenaw. The Portage Lake copper lode was one of the most continuously productive in the region. Keweenaw NHP land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - Keweenaw National Historical Park

mines.name: American Mine (Keweenaw)
mines.description: Copper mine in the Portage Lake district of the Keweenaw. Part of the extensive 19th century copper mining complex. Keweenaw NHP associated land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - Keweenaw National Historical Park

mines.name: Cropper Mine (Marquette)
mines.description: Iron ore mine in the Marquette Iron Range near Negaunee Michigan. Active 1868-1910 as part of the world-famous Lake Superior iron ore district. Michigan DNR public land.

mines.name: Saginaw Mine (Marquette)
mines.description: Iron ore mine in the Marquette Range area of Marquette County Michigan. Active 1874-1910. Michigan DNR public land.

mines.name: Volunteer Mine (Marquette)
mines.description: Iron ore mine in the Marquette Iron Range active during the height of the Lake Superior iron boom 1870-1910. Michigan DNR public land.

mines.name: Mastodon Dome Mine (Fairbanks)
mines.description: Placer gold mine on the slopes of Mastodon Dome north of Fairbanks. Active during the early Fairbanks gold rush era on BLM land.

mines.name: Chatanika Mine (Fairbanks)
mines.description: Placer gold mine on Chatanika River in the northern Fairbanks district. Active 1905-1930 and later dredged on BLM/State land.

mines.name: Kokomo Creek Mine (Nome)
mines.description: Placer gold mine on Kokomo Creek near Nome in the Snake River drainage. Active 1900-1920 on BLM Seward Peninsula land.

mines.name: Penny River Mine No. 2 (Nome)
mines.description: Additional placer gold workings on the Penny River east of Nome. Active 1902-1920 on BLM Seward Peninsula land.

mines.name: Safety Sound Mine (Nome)
mines.description: Placer gold mine near Safety Sound east of Nome on the Seward Peninsula coast. Active 1900-1915 on BLM Alaska land.

mines.name: Solomon Mine (AK)
mines.description: Gold mine near Solomon at the mouth of the Solomon River east of Nome. Part of the extended Nome-area gold district active 1899-1920 on BLM land.

mines.name: Bluff Mine (AK)
mines.description: Placer gold mine near Bluff on the south coast of the Seward Peninsula. Active during the extended Nome rush era 1900-1920 on BLM land.

mines.name: Spruce Creek Mine (Kantishna)
mines.description: Placer gold mine on Spruce Creek in the Kantishna Hills within Denali National Park. Active during the 1905-1920 Kantishna rush on NPS land.

mines.name: Friday Creek Mine (Kantishna)
mines.description: Gold mine on Friday Creek in the Kantishna Hills of Denali National Park. Active during the Kantishna boom on NPS public land.

mines.name: Glen Creek Mine (Kantishna)
mines.description: Placer gold mine on Glen Creek in the Kantishna Hills within Denali National Park. Active during the 1905-1920 Kantishna boom on NPS land.

mines.name: Glacier Creek Mine (Kantishna)
mines.description: Placer gold mine on Glacier Creek in the Kantishna Hills Denali National Park. Active 1906-1920 on NPS land.

mines.name: Moose Creek Mine (Kantishna)
mines.description: Gold mine on Moose Creek in the Kantishna Hills of Denali National Park. Active 1906-1920 on NPS public land.

mines.name: Chatanika Dredge Site
mines.description: Gold dredge site on the Chatanika River north of Fairbanks operated 1927-1962. Large dredge ponds remain on BLM/State land. Second most productive dredge area after Goldstream in the Fairbanks district.

mines.name: Engineer Creek Dredge (Fairbanks)
mines.description: Gold dredge site on Engineer Creek in the Fairbanks district. Operated 1934-1950s on BLM land. Dredge tailings ponds visible from the Steese Highway.

mines.name: Ophir Gold Mine (AK Interior)
mines.description: Gold mine at Ophir ghost town in the Innoko district of interior Alaska along the historic Iditarod Trail. Active 1907-1925 on BLM land.

mines.name: Poorman Creek Mine (AK)
mines.description: Placer gold mine on Poorman Creek in the Ruby district of western interior Alaska. Active 1914-1930 on BLM land near the Yukon River.

mines.name: Ruby Mine (AK)
mines.description: Placer gold mine near Ruby Alaska on the Yukon River. Active during the early 20th century interior Alaska gold rush on BLM land. Ruby is one of the oldest surviving Yukon River communities.

mines.name: Anvik River Mine (AK)
mines.description: Placer gold mine on the Anvik River a Yukon River tributary in western interior Alaska. Active during the early 20th century on BLM land.

mines.name: Dikeman Mine (AK)
mines.description: Gold mine near Dikeman on the Anvik River in western interior Alaska. Active during the early 20th century gold rush era on BLM land.

mines.name: Marshall Mine (AK)
mines.description: Placer gold mine at Marshall also known as Fortuna Ledge on the Yukon Delta area of Alaska. Active 1913-1930 on BLM land in western Alaska.

mines.name: Ophir Creek Mine (Goodnews Bay)
mines.description: Platinum placer mine on Salmon River near Goodnews Bay in southwestern Alaska. The Goodnews Bay area is the largest platinum deposit in North America. Active 1905-1940 on BLM land.

mines.name: Platinum Creek Mine (AK)
mines.description: Platinum placer mine on Salmon River near Goodnews Bay southwestern Alaska. The platinum was discovered in 1914 and the area became the leading platinum producer in North America. BLM public land.

mines.name: Kotlik Mine (AK)
mines.description: Gold mine in western interior Alaska near the Yukon Delta area. Active during the early 20th century gold rush era on BLM land.

mines.name: Unalakleet Mine (AK)
mines.description: Gold mine near Unalakleet on Norton Sound in western Alaska. Active during the Nome-era gold rush expansion on BLM land.

mines.name: Nulato Mine (AK)
mines.description: Gold mine near Nulato on the Yukon River in interior Alaska. Active during the early 20th century gold rush era on BLM land along the Yukon River.

mines.name: Galena Mine (AK)
mines.description: Gold mine near Galena Alaska on the Yukon River. Part of the interior Alaska gold district active in the early 20th century on BLM land.

mines.name: Knik Arm Mine (AK)
mines.description: Gold and copper mine along Knik Arm of Cook Inlet in the Matanuska-Susitna area. Active during the late 1890s Cook Inlet gold rush on BLM land.

mines.name: Willow Creek Mine (AK Matanuska)
mines.description: Gold mine on Willow Creek in the Matanuska-Susitna Valley of southcentral Alaska. Active during the 1897 Cook Inlet area gold rush on BLM land.

mines.name: Hatcher Pass Mine (AK)
mines.description: Gold mine at Hatcher Pass in the Talkeetna Mountains of Matanuska-Susitna Borough Alaska. The Independence Mine State Historical Park preserves 1906-1951 gold mine buildings as a public heritage site.

mines.name: Independence Mine (AK)
mines.description: Major gold mine at Hatcher Pass in the Talkeetna Mountains. Produced significant gold 1906-1951. The Independence Mine State Historical Park preserves the complete mill and bunkhouse complex. Open to public.

mines.name: Fishhook Mine (AK)
mines.description: Gold mine at Fishhook Creek in the Hatcher Pass area of Matanuska-Susitna Borough. Active 1908-1940 near the Independence Mine on State Parks land.

mines.name: Archangel Mine (AK)
mines.description: Gold mine on Archangel Creek in Hatcher Pass area. Active 1910-1940 within the Hatcher Pass management area near the Independence Mine complex. State public land.

mines.name: Talkeetna Mine (AK)
mines.description: Gold mine near Talkeetna at the confluence of the Talkeetna and Susitna Rivers in southcentral Alaska. Active 1914-1930 on BLM land.

mines.name: Resurrection Creek Mine (AK)
mines.description: Placer gold mine on Resurrection Creek on the Kenai Peninsula. The oldest mine still active in Alaska first worked in 1895. Hope Mining Company still operates on BLM land near the ghost town of Hope.

mines.name: Hope Mine (AK)
mines.description: Gold mine at Hope Alaska on Turnagain Arm first worked during the 1896 Cook Inlet gold rush. Hope was a thriving mining camp with thousands of residents in the late 1890s. BLM land on the Kenai Peninsula.

mines.name: Girdwood Mine (AK)
mines.description: Gold mine at Girdwood on Turnagain Arm part of the 1896 Cook Inlet gold rush. The Crow Creek Mine at Girdwood is still open to public gold panning on a private claim while surrounding areas are on BLM land.

mines.name: Ogden Mine (Keweenaw)
mines.description: Copper mine in the Calumet district of the Keweenaw Peninsula. Active during the late 19th century copper boom on Keweenaw NHP associated land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - Keweenaw National Historical Park

mines.name: Winthrop Mine (Keweenaw)
mines.description: Copper mine in the Calumet district of the Keweenaw Peninsula. Active 1869-1895 on Keweenaw NHP associated land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - Keweenaw National Historical Park

mines.name: Cliff Shaft Mine (Keweenaw)
mines.description: Shaft mine in the Cliff Mine complex of the northern Keweenaw. Part of the famous Cliff Mine group on Ottawa National Forest land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - USFS Ottawa National Forest

mines.name: Albion Mine (Keweenaw)
mines.description: Early copper mine in the northern Keweenaw between the Cliff Mine and Central Mine districts. Active 1847-1870 on Ottawa National Forest land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - USFS Ottawa National Forest

mines.name: Mendota Mine (Keweenaw)
mines.description: Early copper mine in the far northern Keweenaw Peninsula near Copper Harbor. Active 1852-1870 on National Forest land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - USFS Ottawa National Forest

mines.name: Sucker Creek Mine No. 2 (OR)
mines.description: Additional gold mine workings on Sucker Creek in the Illinois Valley Josephine County Oregon. Active 1857-1900 on BLM and National Forest land.

mines.name: Galice Mine No. 3 (OR)
mines.description: Third major gold operation in the Galice area Josephine County Oregon on the Rogue River. Part of the long-running Galice mining district active 1857-1920 on BLM land.

mines.name: Greenback Mine (OR Baker)
mines.description: Gold mine near the Greenback community in Baker County Oregon. Part of the Bourne-Sumpter gold district active 1870-1910 on Wallowa-Whitman National Forest land.

mines.name: Rock Creek Mine (OR Josephine)
mines.description: Placer gold mine on Rock Creek in the upper Applegate Valley Jackson County Oregon. Active 1855-1900 on Rogue River-Siskiyou National Forest land.
mines.source: USGS MRDS - Rogue River-Siskiyou National Forest

mines.name: Gold Ray Dam Mine (OR)
mines.description: Hydraulic gold mine site near Gold Ray on the Rogue River in Jackson County Oregon. Active 1896-1905 on BLM land. Gold Ray Dam site is now a state heritage area open to visitors.

mines.name: Table Rock Mine (OR)
mines.description: Gold mine near Table Rock in the Rogue Valley of Jackson County Oregon. Active during the 1854-1880s southwest Oregon gold rush on BLM land.

mines.name: Blackwell Mine (OR)
mines.description: Gold mine in the Sumpter district of Baker County Oregon. Active 1878-1910 on Wallowa-Whitman National Forest public land.
